title: Lao-mon

'''Lao-mon''' was the tropical [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Changeling/Legends|shape-shifting]] [[Shi'ido]] species, it was known in [[Shi'idese|their language]] as '''Sh'shuun'''. Their world remained isolated, its exact location unknown to any outlanders.

==History==
Lao-mon (or Sh'shuun in the [[Shi'idese|native language]])<ref name="NEGAS" /> was an isolated world<ref name="AE" />  within the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] region,<ref name="AJ" /> although some sources place it in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]].<ref name="EAOC" /> This planet was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Shi'ido]], a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Changeling/Legends|shapeshifters]].<ref name="AE" /><ref name="UAA" /><ref name="NEGAS" /><ref name="CE" /> Shi'ido physiology is extremely flexible. In natural form, Shi'ido are roughly [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], with large craniums, thick limbs, and no [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. Their thin [[Bone/Legends|bones]] are very dense, allowing support even in the most awkward mass configurations. Their physiology includes a series of tendons that can release and reattach themselves in different formations. They also have a great deal of hidden fleshy mass that they can access and use to enlarge their shapes. Shi'ido transformations are made complete by the use of [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] suggestions imposed upon viewers, painting the image of what they want the observers to see in their minds. This helps cover over any inaccuracies in the Shi'ido's transformation, but it is extremely difficult to maintain.<ref name="NEGAS" />

[[File:Shiido-UAA.jpg|left|thumb|250px|Lao-mon was home to the sentient Shi'ido species]]
The Shi'ido are a rare species; therefore, galaxy-wide society knows little about them and their homeworld.<ref name="AJ" /><ref name="AE" /><ref name="UAA" /> Their planet, when the Shi'ido speak of it, is referred to as a garden world ravaged by disease,<ref name="AJ" /><ref name="AE" /> but no other information is ever offered.<ref name="AJ" /> The governments of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] have never located Lao-mon<ref name="AE" /> and neither state has any corresponding planet listed in its records.<ref name="AJ" /> The Shi'ido went to great lengths, short of violence, to guard their species' secrets. They were curious, shy, and secretive. They preferred to avoid confrontations, especially on their homeworld, where they would pretend to be innocuous aliens, harmless creatures, boulders, or [[Tree/Legends|trees]] to avoid outlanders. Lao-mon was a tropical world populated by large [[Diet/Legends|predators]]. No expedition to Lao-mon ever yielded evidence that the Shi'ido – or any other sentient species – had ever lived there. It was assumed that the species’ ability to change shape had protected them from both discovery and predators.<ref name="UAA" />

Shi'ido are a long-lived species: some of the oldest members of the race are five hundred [[Standard year/Legends|standard years]] old. For this reason, they've developed an acute interest in learning about the universe and all the people in it-learning something new makes traveling from their solitary world worthwhile.<ref name="NEGAS" /> [[Mammon Hoole]] the Shi'ido was born on Sh'shuun, where he became an excellent student. Eventually, he left his homeworld to study at the [[Galactic Research Academy]], where he was appointed [[Senior Anthropologist|professor of anthropology]].<ref name="GF">''[[Galaxy of Fear: The Nightmare Machine]]''</ref>

[[Lao-mon slider]] was added to the menu at [[Dex's Diner/Legends|Dex's Diner]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. [[Dexter Jettster/Legends|Dexter Jettster]], the host of the diner, chose this name in reference to the Shi'ido's ability to take on any humanoid form.<ref name="DD">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/dexdiner/index.html|text=Dining at Dex's|int=Dining at Dex's}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
This planet is named '''Lao-man''' in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'' and '''Laomon''' in [[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']].
